behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real speech-to-text model output quality (speaker diarization/label preservation). A stubbed transcript would hide real diarization failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior text focuses on one model output property ("speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels"), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT API and assert the returned transcript includes stable speaker labels aligned to utterances used downstream for attendee attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic at a local function/object boundary. Rejected Integration: behavior text is about "Transcript parsing extracts... into the summary input structure" and does not require real module handoff, OS, network, or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixtures containing explicit assignment phrases and assert extracted objects contain correct assignee and task text fields.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: This asserts summary content completeness, which depends on real LLM generation decisions; deterministic stubs can always satisfy the condition and miss real omissions. Rejected Workflow: can be validated with one real summarization pass over prepared transcript context, without requiring multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a real transcript with known per-attendee assignments to the live summarizer (real LLM) and verify each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local routing/mapping logic once decisions are already identified. Rejected Integration: behavior text explicitly scopes to "routes identified decisions" (post-identification), so no real infra or model output is required.
behaviors.test_description: Call the decision-routing mapper with pre-identified decision records and assert they populate only the `decisions` schema fields, not narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is about model extraction/placement quality from transcript content into structured summary sections, which depends on real LLM behavior. Mocked outputs would mask this failure. Rejected Workflow: single summarization decision quality is sufficient to test this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcripts containing explicit decisions through the live LLM summarizer and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This requires multiple sequential agentic steps (segment-level STT/summarization plus merge) and validates cross-step state/handoff coherence. Integration would miss failures in multi-step agentic sequencing. Rejected System: real infra alone is insufficient because correctness depends on chained model outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute long real audio (>60 min) through production segmentation flow, verify ordered segment processing, then validate merged final summary preserves cross-segment continuity and structure.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end pipeline spanning multiple real agentic stages (transcription then summarization) plus orchestration/delivery. Any lower level would miss cross-stage failures and intervention points. Rejected Agentic: behavior text explicitly requires "full pipeline ... completes" across steps, not just single-model correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real meeting recording through production upload-to-delivery flow, assert no human intervention is needed, and verify final structured payload is produced successfully.
